speaker: DUKE
text: My very worthy cousin, fairly met.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Escalus
                .
                 
              Our old and faithful friend, we are glad to see you.

speaker:
ANGELO
              ,
               
              ESCALUS
text: Happy return be to your royal Grace.

speaker: DUKE
text: Many and hearty thankings to you both.

speaker: DUKE
text: We have made inquiry of you, and we hear

speaker: DUKE
text: Such goodness of your justice that our soul

speaker: DUKE
text: Cannot but yield you forth to public thanks,

speaker: DUKE
text: Forerunning more requital.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You make my bonds still greater.

speaker: DUKE
text: O, your desert speaks loud, and I should wrong it

speaker: DUKE
text: To lock it in the wards of covert bosom

speaker: DUKE
text: When it deserves with characters of brass

speaker: DUKE
text: A forted residence ’gainst the tooth of time

speaker: DUKE
text: And razure of oblivion. Give me your hand

speaker: DUKE
text: And let the subject see, to make them know

speaker: DUKE
text: That outward courtesies would fain proclaim

speaker: DUKE
text: Favors that keep within.—Come, Escalus,

speaker: DUKE
text: You must walk by us on our other hand.

speaker: DUKE
text: And good supporters are you.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Now is your time. Speak loud, and kneel before him.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Justice, O royal duke. Vail your regard

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Upon a wronged—I would fain have said, a maid.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O worthy prince, dishonor not your eye

speaker: ISABELLA
text: By throwing it on any other object

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Till you have heard me in my true complaint

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And given me justice, justice, justice, justice.

speaker: DUKE
text: Relate your wrongs. In what, by whom? Be brief.

speaker: DUKE
text: Here is Lord Angelo shall give you justice.

speaker: DUKE
text: Reveal yourself to him.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O worthy duke,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: You bid me seek redemption of the devil.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Hear me yourself, for that which I must speak

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Must either punish me, not being believed,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Or wring redress from you. Hear me, O hear me, here.

speaker: ANGELO
text: My lord, her wits, I fear me, are not firm.

speaker: ANGELO
text: She hath been a suitor to me for her brother

speaker: ANGELO
text: Cut off by course of justice.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: By course of justice!

speaker: ANGELO
text: And she will speak most bitterly and strange.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Most strange, but yet most truly will I speak.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That Angelo’s forsworn, is it not strange?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That Angelo’s a murderer, is ’t not strange?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That Angelo is an adulterous thief,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: An hypocrite, a virgin-violator,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Is it not strange, and strange?

speaker: DUKE
text: Nay, it is ten times strange.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: It is not truer he is Angelo

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Than this is all as true as it is strange.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Nay, it is ten times true, for truth is truth

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To th’ end of reck’ning.

speaker: DUKE
text: Away with her. Poor soul,

speaker: DUKE
text: She speaks this in th’ infirmity of sense.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O prince, I conjure thee, as thou believest

speaker: ISABELLA
text: There is another comfort than this world,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That thou neglect me not with that opinion

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That I am touched with madness. Make not impossible

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That which but seems unlike. ’Tis not impossible

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But one, the wicked’st caitiff on the ground,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: May seem as shy, as grave, as just, as absolute

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As Angelo. Even so may Angelo,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In all his dressings, caracts, titles, forms,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Be an archvillain. Believe it, royal prince,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If he be less, he’s nothing, but he’s more,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Had I more name for badness.

speaker: DUKE
text: By mine honesty,

speaker: DUKE
text: If she be mad—as I believe no other—

speaker: DUKE
text: Her madness hath the oddest frame of sense,

speaker: DUKE
text: Such a dependency of thing on thing,

speaker: DUKE
text: As e’er I heard in madness.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O gracious duke,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Harp not on that; nor do not banish reason

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For inequality, but let your reason serve

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To make the truth appear where it seems hid,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And hide the false seems true.

speaker: DUKE
text: Many that are not mad

speaker: DUKE
text: Have, sure, more lack of reason. What would you say?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am the sister of one Claudio,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Condemned upon the act of fornication

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To lose his head, condemned by Angelo.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I, in probation of a sisterhood,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Was sent to by my brother; one Lucio

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As then the messenger—

speaker: LUCIO
text: That’s I, an ’t like your Grace.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I came to her from Claudio and desired her

speaker: LUCIO
text: To try her gracious fortune with Lord Angelo

speaker: LUCIO
text: For her poor brother’s pardon.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That’s he indeed.

speaker: DUKE
text: You were not bid to speak.

speaker: LUCIO
text: No, my good lord,

speaker: LUCIO
text: Nor wished to hold my peace.

speaker: DUKE
text: I wish you now, then.

speaker: DUKE
text: Pray you take note of it, and when you have

speaker: DUKE
text: A business for yourself, pray heaven you then

speaker: DUKE
text: Be perfect.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I warrant your Honor.

speaker: DUKE
text: The warrant’s for yourself. Take heed to ’t.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: This gentleman told somewhat of my tale.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Right.

speaker: DUKE
text: It may be right, but you are i’ the wrong

speaker: DUKE
text: To speak before your time.—Proceed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I went

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To this pernicious caitiff deputy—

speaker: DUKE
text: That’s somewhat madly spoken.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Pardon it;

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The phrase is to the matter.

speaker: DUKE
text: Mended again. The matter; proceed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In brief, to set the needless process by:

speaker: ISABELLA
text: How I persuaded, how I prayed and kneeled,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: How he refelled me, and how I replied—

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For this was of much length—the vile conclusion

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I now begin with grief and shame to utter.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: He would not, but by gift of my chaste body

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To his concupiscible intemperate lust,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Release my brother; and after much debatement,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: My sisterly remorse confutes mine honor,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And I did yield to him. But the next morn betimes,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: His purpose surfeiting, he sends a warrant

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For my poor brother’s head.

speaker: DUKE
text: This is most likely!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, that it were as like as it is true!

speaker: DUKE
text: By heaven, fond wretch, thou know’st not what thou speak’st,

speaker: DUKE
text: Or else thou art suborned against his honor

speaker: DUKE
text: In hateful practice. First, his integrity

speaker: DUKE
text: Stands without blemish; next, it imports no reason

speaker: DUKE
text: That with such vehemency he should pursue

speaker: DUKE
text: Faults proper to himself. If he had so offended,

speaker: DUKE
text: He would have weighed thy brother by himself

speaker: DUKE
text: And not have cut him off. Someone hath set you on.

speaker: DUKE
text: Confess the truth, and say by whose advice

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou cam’st here to complain.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And is this all?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Then, O you blessèd ministers above,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Keep me in patience, and with ripened time

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Unfold the evil which is here wrapped up

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In countenance. Heaven shield your Grace from woe,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As I, thus wronged, hence unbelievèd go.

speaker: DUKE
text: I know you’d fain be gone.—An officer!

speaker: DUKE
text: To prison with her. Shall we thus permit

speaker: DUKE
text: A blasting and a scandalous breath to fall

speaker: DUKE
text: On him so near us? This needs must be a practice.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Who knew of your intent and coming hither?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: One that I would were here, Friar Lodowick.

speaker: DUKE
text: A ghostly father, belike. Who knows that Lodowick?

speaker: LUCIO
text: My lord, I know him. ’Tis a meddling friar.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I do not like the man. Had he been lay, my lord,

speaker: LUCIO
text: For certain words he spake against your Grace

speaker: LUCIO
text: In your retirement, I had swinged him soundly.

speaker: DUKE
text: Words against me? This’ a good friar, belike.

speaker: DUKE
text: And to set on this wretched woman here

speaker: DUKE
text: Against our substitute! Let this friar be found.

speaker: LUCIO
text: But yesternight, my lord, she and that friar,

speaker: LUCIO
text: I saw them at the prison. A saucy friar,

speaker: LUCIO
text: A very scurvy fellow.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Blessed be your royal Grace.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: I have stood by, my lord, and I have heard

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Your royal ear abused. First hath this woman

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Most wrongfully accused your substitute,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Who is as free from touch or soil with her

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: As she from one ungot.

speaker: DUKE
text: We did believe no less.

speaker: DUKE
text: Know you that Friar Lodowick that she speaks of?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: I know him for a man divine and holy,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Not scurvy, nor a temporary meddler,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: As he’s reported by this gentleman;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: And on my trust, a man that never yet

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Did, as he vouches, misreport your Grace.

speaker: LUCIO
text: My lord, most villainously, believe it.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Well, he in time may come to clear himself;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: But at this instant he is sick, my lord,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Of a strange fever. Upon his mere request,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Being come to knowledge that there was complaint

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Intended ’gainst Lord Angelo, came I hither

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: To speak as from his mouth, what he doth know

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Is true and false, and what he with his oath

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: And all probation will make up full clear

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Whensoever he’s convented. First, for this woman,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: To justify this worthy nobleman,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: So vulgarly and personally accused,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Her shall you hear disprovèd to her eyes

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Till she herself confess it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Good friar, let’s hear it.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Do you not smile at this, Lord Angelo?

speaker: DUKE
text: O heaven, the vanity of wretched fools!—

speaker: DUKE
text: Give us some seats.—Come, cousin Angelo,

speaker: DUKE
text: In this I’ll be impartial. Be you judge

speaker: DUKE
text: Of your own cause.

speaker: DUKE
text: Is this the witness, friar?

speaker: DUKE
text: First, let her show her face, and after speak.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Pardon, my lord, I will not show my face

speaker: MARIANA
text: Until my husband bid me.

speaker: DUKE
text: What, are you married?

speaker: MARIANA
text: No, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Are you a maid?

speaker: MARIANA
text: No, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: A widow, then?

speaker: MARIANA
text: Neither, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why you are nothing, then, neither maid, widow,

speaker: DUKE
text: nor wife?

speaker: LUCIO
text: My lord, she may be a punk, for many of them

speaker: LUCIO
text: are neither maid, widow, nor wife.

speaker: DUKE
text: Silence that fellow. I would he had some cause

speaker: DUKE
text: to prattle for himself.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Well, my lord.

speaker: MARIANA
text: My lord, I do confess I ne’er was married,

speaker: MARIANA
text: And I confess besides I am no maid.

speaker: MARIANA
text: I have known my husband, yet my husband

speaker: MARIANA
text: Knows not that ever he knew me.

speaker: LUCIO
text: He was drunk, then, my lord; it can be no better.

speaker: DUKE
text: For the benefit of silence, would thou wert so

speaker: DUKE
text: too.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Well, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: This is no witness for Lord Angelo.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Now I come to ’t, my lord.

speaker: MARIANA
text: She that accuses him of fornication

speaker: MARIANA
text: In selfsame manner doth accuse my husband,

speaker: MARIANA
text: And charges him, my lord, with such a time

speaker: MARIANA
text: When, I’ll depose, I had him in mine arms

speaker: MARIANA
text: With all th’ effect of love.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Charges she more than me?

speaker: MARIANA
text: Not that I know.

speaker: DUKE
text: No? You say your husband.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Why, just, my lord, and that is Angelo,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Who thinks he knows that he ne’er knew my body,

speaker: MARIANA
text: But knows, he thinks, that he knows Isabel’s.

speaker: ANGELO
text: This is a strange abuse. Let’s see thy face.

speaker: MARIANA
text: My husband bids me. Now I will unmask.

speaker: MARIANA
text: This is that face, thou cruel Angelo,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Which once thou swor’st was worth the looking on.

speaker: MARIANA
text: This is the hand which, with a vowed contract,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Was fast belocked in thine. This is the body

speaker: MARIANA
text: That took away the match from Isabel

speaker: MARIANA
text: And did supply thee at thy garden house

speaker: MARIANA
text: In her imagined person.

speaker: DUKE
text: Know you this woman?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Carnally, she says.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sirrah, no more.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Enough, my lord.

speaker: ANGELO
text: My lord, I must confess I know this woman,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And five years since there was some speech of marriage

speaker: ANGELO
text: Betwixt myself and her, which was broke off,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Partly for that her promisèd proportions

speaker: ANGELO
text: Came short of composition, but in chief

speaker: ANGELO
text: For that her reputation was disvalued

speaker: ANGELO
text: In levity. Since which time of five years

speaker: ANGELO
text: I never spake with her, saw her, nor heard from her,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Upon my faith and honor.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Noble prince,

speaker: MARIANA
text: As there comes light from heaven and words from breath,

speaker: MARIANA
text: As there is sense in truth and truth in virtue,

speaker: MARIANA
text: I am affianced this man’s wife as strongly

speaker: MARIANA
text: As words could make up vows. And, my good lord,

speaker: MARIANA
text: But Tuesday night last gone in ’s garden house

speaker: MARIANA
text: He knew me as a wife. As this is true,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Let me in safety raise me from my knees,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Or else forever be confixèd here

speaker: MARIANA
text: A marble monument.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I did but smile till now.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Now, good my lord, give me the scope of justice.

speaker: ANGELO
text: My patience here is touched. I do perceive

speaker: ANGELO
text: These poor informal women are no more

speaker: ANGELO
text: But instruments of some more mightier member

speaker: ANGELO
text: That sets them on. Let me have way, my lord,

speaker: ANGELO
text: To find this practice out.

speaker: DUKE
text: Ay, with my heart,

speaker: DUKE
text: And punish them to your height of pleasure.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou foolish friar, and thou pernicious woman,

speaker: DUKE
text: Compact with her that’s gone, think’st thou thy oaths,

speaker: DUKE
text: Though they would swear down each particular saint,

speaker: DUKE
text: Were testimonies against his worth and credit

speaker: DUKE
text: That’s sealed in approbation?—You, Lord Escalus,

speaker: DUKE
text: Sit with my cousin; lend him your kind pains

speaker: DUKE
text: To find out this abuse, whence ’tis derived.

speaker: DUKE
text: There is another friar that set them on.

speaker: DUKE
text: Let him be sent for.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Would he were here, my lord, for he indeed

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Hath set the women on to this complaint;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: Your provost knows the place where he abides,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              PETER
text: And he may fetch him.

speaker: DUKE
text: Go, do it instantly.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Angelo
                .
                 
              And you, my noble and well-warranted cousin,

speaker: DUKE
text: Whom it concerns to hear this matter forth,

speaker: DUKE
text: Do with your injuries as seems you best

speaker: DUKE
text: In any chastisement. I for a while

speaker: DUKE
text: Will leave you; but stir not you till you have

speaker: DUKE
text: Well determined upon these slanderers.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: My lord, we’ll do it throughly.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Signior Lucio, did not you say you knew that Friar

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Lodowick to be a dishonest person?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Cucullus non facit monachum, honest in nothing

speaker: LUCIO
text: but in his clothes, and one that hath spoke most

speaker: LUCIO
text: villainous speeches of the Duke.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: We shall entreat you to abide here till he

speaker: ESCALUS
text: come, and enforce them against him. We shall find

speaker: ESCALUS
text: this friar a notable fellow.

speaker: LUCIO
text: As any in Vienna, on my word.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Call that same Isabel here once again. I would

speaker: ESCALUS
text: speak with her.

speaker: ESCALUS
text:
To
                 
                Angelo
                .
               Pray you, my lord, give me leave to

speaker: ESCALUS
text: question. You shall see how I’ll handle her.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Not better than he, by her own report.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Say you?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Marry, sir, I think, if you handled her privately,

speaker: LUCIO
text: she would sooner confess; perchance publicly she’ll

speaker: LUCIO
text: be ashamed.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: I will go darkly to work with her.

speaker: LUCIO
text: That’s the way, for women are light at midnight.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Come on, mistress. Here’s a gentlewoman

speaker: ESCALUS
text: denies all that you have said.

speaker: LUCIO
text: My lord, here comes the rascal I spoke of, here

speaker: LUCIO
text: with the Provost.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: In very good time. Speak not you to him till

speaker: ESCALUS
text: we call upon you.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Mum.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Come, sir, did you set

speaker: ESCALUS
text: these women on to slander Lord Angelo? They have

speaker: ESCALUS
text: confessed you did.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis false.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: How? Know you where you are?

speaker: DUKE
text: Respect to your great place, and let the devil

speaker: DUKE
text: Be sometime honored for his burning throne.

speaker: DUKE
text: Where is the Duke? ’Tis he should hear me speak.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: The Duke’s in us, and we will hear you speak.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Look you speak justly.

speaker: DUKE
text: Boldly, at least.—But, O, poor souls,

speaker: DUKE
text: Come you to seek the lamb here of the fox?

speaker: DUKE
text: Good night to your redress. Is the Duke gone?

speaker: DUKE
text: Then is your cause gone too. The Duke’s unjust

speaker: DUKE
text: Thus to retort your manifest appeal,

speaker: DUKE
text: And put your trial in the villain’s mouth

speaker: DUKE
text: Which here you come to accuse.

speaker: LUCIO
text: This is the rascal; this is he I spoke of.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Why, thou unreverend and unhallowed friar,

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Is ’t not enough thou hast suborned these women

speaker: ESCALUS
text: To accuse this worthy man, but, in foul mouth

speaker: ESCALUS
text: And in the witness of his proper ear,

speaker: ESCALUS
text: To call him villain? And then to glance from him

speaker: ESCALUS
text: To th’ Duke himself, to tax him with injustice?—

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Take him hence. To th’ rack with him. We’ll touse him

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Joint by joint, but we will know his purpose.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: What? “Unjust”?

speaker: DUKE
text: Be not so hot. The Duke

speaker: DUKE
text: Dare no more stretch this finger of mine than he

speaker: DUKE
text: Dare rack his own. His subject am I not,

speaker: DUKE
text: Nor here provincial. My business in this state

speaker: DUKE
text: Made me a looker-on here in Vienna,

speaker: DUKE
text: Where I have seen corruption boil and bubble

speaker: DUKE
text: Till it o’errun the stew. Laws for all faults,

speaker: DUKE
text: But faults so countenanced that the strong statutes

speaker: DUKE
text: Stand like the forfeits in a barber’s shop,

speaker: DUKE
text: As much in mock as mark.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Slander to th’ state!

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Away with him to prison.

speaker: ANGELO
text: What can you vouch against him, Signior Lucio?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Is this the man that you did tell us of?

speaker: LUCIO
text: ’Tis he, my lord.—Come hither, Goodman Baldpate.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Do you know me?

speaker: DUKE
text: I remember you, sir, by the sound of

speaker: DUKE
text: your voice. I met you at the prison in the absence of

speaker: DUKE
text: the Duke.

speaker: LUCIO
text: O, did you so? And do you remember what you

speaker: LUCIO
text: said of the Duke?

speaker: DUKE
text: Most notedly, sir.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Do you so, sir? And was the Duke a fleshmonger,

speaker: LUCIO
text: a fool, and a coward, as you then reported him to

speaker: LUCIO
text: be?

speaker: DUKE
text: You must, sir, change persons with me

speaker: DUKE
text: ere you make that my report. You indeed spoke so

speaker: DUKE
text: of him, and much more, much worse.

speaker: LUCIO
text: O, thou damnable fellow! Did not I pluck thee by

speaker: LUCIO
text: the nose for thy speeches?

speaker: DUKE
text: I protest I love the Duke as I love

speaker: DUKE
text: myself.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Hark how the villain would close now, after

speaker: ANGELO
text: his treasonable abuses!

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Such a fellow is not to be talked withal. Away

speaker: ESCALUS
text: with him to prison. Where is the Provost?

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Away with him to prison. Lay bolts

speaker: ESCALUS
text: enough upon him. Let him speak no more. Away

speaker: ESCALUS
text: with those giglets too, and with the other confederate

speaker: ESCALUS
text: companion.

speaker: DUKE
text: Stay, sir, stay awhile.

speaker: ANGELO
text: What, resists he?—Help him, Lucio.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Come, sir, come, sir,

speaker: LUCIO
text: come, sir. Foh, sir! Why you bald-pated, lying rascal,

speaker: LUCIO
text: you must be hooded, must you? Show your knave’s

speaker: LUCIO
text: visage, with a pox to you! Show your sheep-biting

speaker: LUCIO
text: face, and be hanged an hour! Will ’t not off?

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou art the first knave that e’er mad’st a duke.—

speaker: DUKE
text: First, provost, let me bail these gentle three.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Lucio
                .
                 
              Sneak not away, sir, for the friar and you

speaker: DUKE
text: Must have a word anon.—Lay hold on him.

speaker: LUCIO
text: This may prove worse than hanging.

speaker: DUKE
text: What you have spoke I pardon. Sit you down.

speaker: DUKE
text:
We’ll borrow place of him.
                 
                To
                 
                Angelo
                .
               Sir, by your leave.

speaker: DUKE
text: Hast thou or word, or wit, or impudence

speaker: DUKE
text: That yet can do thee office? If thou hast,

speaker: DUKE
text: Rely upon it till my tale be heard,

speaker: DUKE
text: And hold no longer out.

speaker: ANGELO
text: O my dread lord,

speaker: ANGELO
text: I should be guiltier than my guiltiness

speaker: ANGELO
text: To think I can be undiscernible,

speaker: ANGELO
text: When I perceive your Grace, like power divine,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Hath looked upon my passes. Then, good prince,

speaker: ANGELO
text: No longer session hold upon my shame,

speaker: ANGELO
text: But let my trial be mine own confession.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Immediate sentence then and sequent death

speaker: ANGELO
text: Is all the grace I beg.

speaker: DUKE
text: Come hither, Mariana.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Angelo
                .
                 
              Say, wast thou e’er contracted to this woman?

speaker: ANGELO
text: I was, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Go take her hence and marry her instantly.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Friar
                 
                Peter
                .
                 
              Do you the office, friar, which consummate,

speaker: DUKE
text: Return him here again.—Go with him, provost.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: My lord, I am more amazed at his dishonor

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Than at the strangeness of it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Come hither, Isabel.

speaker: DUKE
text: Your friar is now your prince. As I was then

speaker: DUKE
text: Advertising and holy to your business,

speaker: DUKE
text: Not changing heart with habit, I am still

speaker: DUKE
text: Attorneyed at your service.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, give me pardon

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That I, your vassal, have employed and pained

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Your unknown sovereignty.

speaker: DUKE
text: You are pardoned, Isabel.

speaker: DUKE
text: And now, dear maid, be you as free to us.

speaker: DUKE
text: Your brother’s death, I know, sits at your heart,

speaker: DUKE
text: And you may marvel why I obscured myself,

speaker: DUKE
text: Laboring to save his life, and would not rather

speaker: DUKE
text: Make rash remonstrance of my hidden power

speaker: DUKE
text: Than let him so be lost. O most kind maid,

speaker: DUKE
text: It was the swift celerity of his death,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which I did think with slower foot came on,

speaker: DUKE
text: That brained my purpose. But peace be with him.

speaker: DUKE
text: That life is better life past fearing death

speaker: DUKE
text: Than that which lives to fear. Make it your comfort,

speaker: DUKE
text: So happy is your brother.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I do, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: For this new-married man approaching here,

speaker: DUKE
text: Whose salt imagination yet hath wronged

speaker: DUKE
text: Your well-defended honor, you must pardon

speaker: DUKE
text: For Mariana’s sake. But as he adjudged your brother—

speaker: DUKE
text: Being criminal in double violation

speaker: DUKE
text: Of sacred chastity and of promise-breach

speaker: DUKE
text: Thereon dependent for your brother’s life—

speaker: DUKE
text: The very mercy of the law cries out

speaker: DUKE
text: Most audible, even from his proper tongue,

speaker: DUKE
text: “An Angelo for Claudio, death for death.”

speaker: DUKE
text: Haste still pays haste, and leisure answers leisure;

speaker: DUKE
text: Like doth quit like, and measure still for measure.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Then, Angelo, thy fault’s thus manifested,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which, though thou wouldst deny, denies thee vantage.

speaker: DUKE
text: We do condemn thee to the very block

speaker: DUKE
text: Where Claudio stooped to death, and with like haste.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Away with him.

speaker: MARIANA
text: O my most gracious lord,

speaker: MARIANA
text: I hope you will not mock me with a husband.

speaker: DUKE
text: It is your husband mocked you with a husband.

speaker: DUKE
text: Consenting to the safeguard of your honor,

speaker: DUKE
text: I thought your marriage fit. Else imputation,

speaker: DUKE
text: For that he knew you, might reproach your life

speaker: DUKE
text: And choke your good to come. For his possessions,

speaker: DUKE
text: Although by confiscation they are ours,

speaker: DUKE
text: We do instate and widow you with all

speaker: DUKE
text: To buy you a better husband.

speaker: MARIANA
text: O my dear lord,

speaker: MARIANA
text: I crave no other nor no better man.

speaker: DUKE
text: Never crave him. We are definitive.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Gentle my liege—

speaker: DUKE
text: You do but lose your labor.—

speaker: DUKE
text:
Away with him to death.
                 
                To
                 
                Lucio
                .
               Now, sir, to you.

speaker: MARIANA
text: O, my good lord.—Sweet Isabel, take my part.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Lend me your knees, and all my life to come

speaker: MARIANA
text: I’ll lend you all my life to do you service.

speaker: DUKE
text: Against all sense you do importune her.

speaker: DUKE
text: Should she kneel down in mercy of this fact,

speaker: DUKE
text: Her brother’s ghost his pavèd bed would break

speaker: DUKE
text: And take her hence in horror.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Isabel,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Sweet Isabel, do yet but kneel by me,

speaker: MARIANA
text: Hold up your hands, say nothing. I’ll speak all.

speaker: MARIANA
text: They say best men are molded out of faults,

speaker: MARIANA
text: And, for the most, become much more the better

speaker: MARIANA
text: For being a little bad. So may my husband.

speaker: MARIANA
text: O Isabel, will you not lend a knee?

speaker: DUKE
text: He dies for Claudio’s death.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Most bounteous sir,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Look, if it please you, on this man condemned

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As if my brother lived. I partly think

speaker: ISABELLA
text: A due sincerity governed his deeds

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Till he did look on me. Since it is so,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Let him not die. My brother had but justice,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: In that he did the thing for which he died.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For Angelo,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: His act did not o’ertake his bad intent,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And must be buried but as an intent

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That perished by the way. Thoughts are no subjects,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Intents but merely thoughts.

speaker: MARIANA
text: Merely, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Your suit’s unprofitable. Stand up, I say.

speaker: DUKE
text: I have bethought me of another fault.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Provost, how came it Claudio was beheaded

speaker: DUKE
text: At an unusual hour?

speaker: PROVOST
text: It was commanded so.

speaker: DUKE
text: Had you a special warrant for the deed?

speaker: PROVOST
text: No, my good lord, it was by private message.

speaker: DUKE
text: For which I do discharge you of your office.

speaker: DUKE
text: Give up your keys.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Pardon me, noble lord.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I thought it was a fault, but knew it not,

speaker: PROVOST
text: Yet did repent me after more advice,

speaker: PROVOST
text: For testimony whereof, one in the prison

speaker: PROVOST
text: That should by private order else have died,

speaker: PROVOST
text: I have reserved alive.

speaker: DUKE
text: What’s he?

speaker: PROVOST
text: His name is Barnardine.

speaker: DUKE
text: I would thou hadst done so by Claudio.

speaker: DUKE
text: Go fetch him hither. Let me look upon him.

speaker: ESCALUS
text: I am sorry one so learnèd and so wise

speaker: ESCALUS
text: As you, Lord Angelo, have still appeared,

speaker: ESCALUS
text: Should slip so grossly, both in the heat of blood

speaker: ESCALUS
text: And lack of tempered judgment afterward.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I am sorry that such sorrow I procure;

speaker: ANGELO
text: And so deep sticks it in my penitent heart

speaker: ANGELO
text: That I crave death more willingly than mercy.

speaker: ANGELO
text: ’Tis my deserving, and I do entreat it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Which is that Barnardine?

speaker: PROVOST
text: This, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: There was a friar told me of this man.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Sirrah, thou art said to have a stubborn soul

speaker: DUKE
text: That apprehends no further than this world,

speaker: DUKE
text: And squar’st thy life according. Thou ’rt condemned.

speaker: DUKE
text: But, for those earthly faults, I quit them all,

speaker: DUKE
text: And pray thee take this mercy to provide

speaker: DUKE
text: For better times to come.—Friar, advise him.

speaker: DUKE
text: I leave him to your hand.—What muffled fellow’s that?

speaker: PROVOST
text: This is another prisoner that I saved

speaker: PROVOST
text: Who should have died when Claudio lost his head,

speaker: PROVOST
text: As like almost to Claudio as himself.

speaker: DUKE
text: If he be like your brother, for his sake

speaker: DUKE
text: Is he pardoned; and for your lovely sake,

speaker: DUKE
text: Give me your hand and say you will be mine,

speaker: DUKE
text: He is my brother too. But fitter time for that.

speaker: DUKE
text: By this Lord Angelo perceives he’s safe;

speaker: DUKE
text: Methinks I see a quick’ning in his eye.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Well, Angelo, your evil quits you well.

speaker: DUKE
text: Look that you love your wife, her worth worth yours.

speaker: DUKE
text: I find an apt remission in myself.

speaker: DUKE
text: And yet here’s one in place I cannot pardon.

speaker: DUKE
text:
To
                 
                Lucio
                .
                 
              You, sirrah, that knew me for a fool, a coward,

speaker: DUKE
text: One all of luxury, an ass, a madman.

speaker: DUKE
text: Wherein have I so deserved of you

speaker: DUKE
text: That you extol me thus?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Faith, my lord, I spoke it but according to the

speaker: LUCIO
text: trick. If you will hang me for it, you may, but I had

speaker: LUCIO
text: rather it would please you I might be whipped.

speaker: DUKE
text: Whipped first, sir, and hanged after.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Proclaim it, provost, round about the city,

speaker: DUKE
text: If any woman wronged by this lewd fellow—

speaker: DUKE
text: As I have heard him swear himself there’s one

speaker: DUKE
text: Whom he begot with child—let her appear,

speaker: DUKE
text: And he shall marry her. The nuptial finished,

speaker: DUKE
text: Let him be whipped and hanged.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I beseech your Highness do not marry me to a

speaker: LUCIO
text: whore. Your Highness said even now I made you a

speaker: LUCIO
text: duke. Good my lord, do not recompense me in

speaker: LUCIO
text: making me a cuckold.

speaker: DUKE
text: Upon mine honor, thou shalt marry her.

speaker: DUKE
text: Thy slanders I forgive and therewithal

speaker: DUKE
text: Remit thy other forfeits.—Take him to prison,

speaker: DUKE
text: And see our pleasure herein executed.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Marrying a punk, my lord, is pressing to death,

speaker: LUCIO
text: whipping, and hanging.

speaker: DUKE
text: Slandering a prince deserves it.

speaker: DUKE
text: She, Claudio, that you wronged, look you restore.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Joy to you, Mariana.—Love her, Angelo.

speaker: DUKE
text: I have confessed her, and I know her virtue.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Thanks, good friend Escalus, for thy much goodness.

speaker: DUKE
text: There’s more behind that is more gratulate.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Thanks, provost, for thy care and secrecy.

speaker: DUKE
text: We shall employ thee in a worthier place.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Forgive him, Angelo, that brought you home

speaker: DUKE
text: The head of Ragozine for Claudio’s.

speaker: DUKE
text: Th’ offense pardons itself.—Dear Isabel,

speaker: DUKE
text: I have a motion much imports your good,

speaker: DUKE
text: Whereto if you’ll a willing ear incline,

speaker: DUKE
text: What’s mine is yours, and what is yours is mine.—

speaker: DUKE
text: So, bring us to our palace, where we’ll show

speaker: DUKE
text: What’s yet behind that’s meet you all should know.
